title: Does the cyclic group C_3439 admit a (3439,1719,859)-difference set?
micro_paper_assessment: The target is still micro-paper eligible, but this solve pass only reached a structural quotient packet: exact C_19 and C_181 count systems, forced parity reductions, and a conditional two-pattern collapse on the C_19 quotient if the contracted multiplier theorem applies at w = 19. That is theorem-facing but still too thin for publication.
family_affinity: high; exact residual cyclic Hadamard case with a quotient-character, parity, and potential contracted-multiplier obstruction profile
generalization_signal: moderate; the quotient-count and parity-reduction template could transfer to other composite-order residual cyclic Hadamard cases with small factors
candidate_theorem_slice: Unconditionally, any (3439,1719,859) cyclic difference set forces odd C_19 quotient deviations and even C_181 quotient deviations satisfying exact reduced autocorrelation systems; conditionally on the Baumert-Gordon w=19 multiplier theorem, the C_19 quotient counts collapse to (63,93,91) or (117,91,87) up to orbit swap.
next_action: verify the exact contracted multiplier theorem hypotheses at w = 19 and then combine the two allowed C_19 patterns with the C_181 quotient constraints or a full 19x181 incidence argument
